message:
feat(core): streaming join preparation/emission split, pager page-cache overhaul, btree cursor range extensions, and type coercion hardening

Connection layer:
- Split try_stream_simple_join_select_rows into a prepare phase
  (prepare_simple_join_select_rows_with_scanner) and an emit phase
  (emit_prepared_simple_join_rows), enabling callers to decouple row
  scanning from projection/emission and supporting pluggable scan
  sources via a generic scanner closure
- Extract active_txn_is_open_or_borrowed() helper to consolidate
  RefCell borrow-or-assume-active logic across transactional checks
- Add can_preserve_existing_live_vtabs_after_reload() to gate live
  virtual-table reuse on matching schema cookie AND local commit seq,
  preventing stale vtab instances after external writer DDL
- is_live_vtab check factored out of the join preparation hot path
  into a callback parameter for testability

Pager:
- Major page cache refactor (~1400 lines) — expanded cache management,
  eviction, and dirty-page tracking infrastructure

B-tree cursor:
- Extended cursor operations with additional range-scan support

Types (record.rs, value.rs):
- Hardened type coercion and record serialization paths with additional
  edge-case handling for mixed-type comparisons and NULL propagation

VDBE engine:
- Expanded opcode handling for the new join preparation flow

WAL:
- group_commit and lib adjustments for the refactored pager interface

Tests:
- New correctness_mixed_dml and correctness_transactions e2e test
  expansions covering the streaming join and transaction edge cases
- New bd_obixt_track_g_fast_path e2e test for optimized scan paths
- Updated planner index selection artifact for changed query plans

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
